blueprint.name: Smart Home Battery
blueprint.description: Generic Home Assistant blueprint for up to four batteries. It allocates discharge from house demand, can trigger opportunistic charging during real export, stops managed discharge during off-peak periods, and writes a signed power target for each battery into a numeric entity, with optional custom actions for integrations that need a separate mode switch.
message.invalid_sensor: House power sensor {{ house_power_sensor }} is unavailable or not numeric.
message.small_delta_skipped: House power sensor delta below trigger threshold:
trace.stop_small_delta: Stop on small house power delta
validation.no_target_entity.suffix: is enabled by its state of charge sensor, but no Target power entity is configured. Add a `number` or `input_number` entity, or clear the state of charge sensor to disable this battery slot.
validation.no_power.suffix: has a target power entity configured, but both maximum power limits are set to 0 W. Set a non-zero Maximum discharge power or Maximum charge power to make this slot usable.
input.house_power_sensor.name: House power sensor
input.house_power_sensor.description: Main house power sensor. This selector only shows power sensors. Use a signed sensor in watts with positive values for import and negative values for export.
input.off_peak_entities.name: Active off-peak entities
input.off_peak_entities.description: Optional entities that turn on during off-peak periods. If at least one selected entity stays on for 15 seconds, the blueprint stops commanding discharge and runs the configured night charge actions. Leaving on is handled immediately.
input.battery.soc_sensor.name: State of charge sensor
input.battery.soc_sensor.description: Leave empty to ignore this battery slot. Otherwise, this selector only shows battery sensors that report a charge percentage, and the chosen sensor enables the slot. An enabled slot must also expose a numeric target entity and at least one non-zero power limit.
input.battery.max_discharge_w.name: Maximum discharge power
input.battery.max_discharge_w.descriptio: Manual discharge limit used by the allocator for this battery.
input.battery.min_discharge_soc_pct.name: Minimum state of charge for discharge
input.battery.min_discharge_soc_pct.desc: Minimum state of charge required before this battery can be assigned discharge power. If the battery is below this threshold, the blueprint removes it from discharge allocation and writes 0 if it still has an active discharge target.
input.battery.max_charge_w.name: Maximum charge power
input.battery.max_charge_w.description: Manual charge limit used by the allocator for opportunistic charging from real export. Set to 0 if this battery must never be charged by the blueprint.
input.battery.reactive.name: Reactive battery
input.battery.reactive.description: Enable this option to make the battery reactive: it will be adjusted frequently to absorb residual demand changes and recover export as soon as possible. Leave it off (default) for stable behavior where the battery keeps a steady target and lets reactive batteries handle the variations.
input.battery.cooldown_seconds.name: Command cooldown
input.battery.cooldown_seconds.descripti: Minimum delay between two active target updates for this battery. A short cooldown works best with reactive batteries so they can quickly absorb residual changes. A longer cooldown keeps the target steadier. Set it to 0 to disable the delay entirely. Writing `0` can still happen immediately so the blueprint can stop a battery for safety. Reactive batteries can restart from `0` immediately when the raw house meter confirms matching import or export. Non-reactive batteries wait for their cooldown before restarting from `0`, while raw export can still reduce an active discharge target before the cooldown expires.
input.battery.target_power_entity.name: Target power entity
input.battery.target_power_entity.descri: The `number` or `input_number` entity written by the blueprint. The target is signed in watts: positive for discharge, negative for charge, `0` for stop. If this battery should charge, the selected entity must accept negative values.
input.battery.target_power_uses_absolute: Enable this when the target entity expects a positive power magnitude for both charge and discharge. The blueprint keeps its internal signed target, but writes the absolute value to this entity. Use charge and discharge actions to select the battery mode when the integration needs a separate direction control.
input.battery.actual_power_sensor.name: Actual power sensor
input.battery.actual_power_sensor.descri: Optional power sensor that reports the battery's real output in watts. The allocator still uses the target entity and the house meter as its primary control loop. After the response grace period, a valid non-stale reading is used only as a guardrail to avoid counting a battery as real supply when it is no longer delivering. A stale reading does not prevent an idle battery from starting. If telemetry stays stale after an active target had time to react, that battery is kept out of extra discharge allocation while its already active target can stay in place unless a stop, sign change, or real export requires changing it.
input.battery.discharge_actions.name: Discharge actions
input.battery.discharge_actions.descript: Optional custom actions executed when this battery should actively discharge, and again when it should stop but still reports physical discharge. They run on every active discharge target update. Variables such as battery_slot, battery_soc, target_power_w, target_discharge_w, target_charge_w, house_power_w, import_need_w and export_surplus_w are available inside these actions; target variables are initialized globally and then overwritten with the current slot values before this sequence runs.
input.battery.charge_actions.name: Charge actions
input.battery.charge_actions.description: Optional custom actions executed when this battery should actively charge from surplus export, and again when it should stop but still reports physical charge. They run on every active charge target update. Variables such as battery_slot, battery_soc, target_power_w, target_discharge_w, target_charge_w, house_power_w, import_need_w and export_surplus_w are available inside these actions; target variables are initialized globally and then overwritten with the current slot values before this sequence runs.
input.battery.night_charge_actions.name: Night charge actions
input.battery.night_charge_actions.descr: Optional custom actions executed when off-peak periods become active, and after Home Assistant start or automation reload if an off-peak period is already active. Use them to put this battery in its overnight charging mode. Variables such as battery_slot, battery_soc, target_power_w, target_discharge_w, target_charge_w, house_power_w, import_need_w, export_surplus_w and off_peak_active are available inside these actions.
